description_long: This sculpture depicts the bust of a bearded man wearing a conical helmet adorned with a band and detailed curls around the head. The facial features are finely rendered, with a prominent nose, deep-set eyes, and a full beard and mustache. The helmet is tall and tapers to a rounded point, suggesting a historical or mythological figure, possibly from ancient Greece or Rome. The bust is mounted on a simple, square stone or marble base, with a visible metal support rod connecting the sculpture to the base. The surface of the sculpture has a weathered, patinated bronze appearance, with greenish and brownish hues indicating age and oxidation. The back of the head is also detailed, with the helmet and hair rendered in relief, and the overall form is compact and solid.
description: A detailed bronze bust of a bearded man in a conical helmet, mounted on a square stone base with a weathered patina. The sculpture features intricate facial details and textured hair.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full face, helmet, and detailed beard of the bust.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the profile of the face, helmet, and the pronounced curls of the hair.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back of the helmet and the rounded, textured hair.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the profile of the face, helmet, and the detailed beard and hair.
attribute_subject_matter: human figure wearing a conical helmet
attribute_location_context: The sculpture is displayed on a plain, square base with no visible environmental context, suggesting it could be placed in a gallery, museum, or outdoor urban setting.
